dir=\"auto\"\u003eAgent Zero can use a Kali Linux system to accomplish your task. It can inspect files, run commands, write code, install and use tools, create scripts, search the web, and adapt its workflow as the task evolves.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe important idea is not a fixed list of buttons. The important idea is that the agent can build and use the right tool when the work demands it.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eUniversal Canvas\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-universal-canvas\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Universal Canvas\" href=\"#universal-canvas\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAgent Zero is becoming more visual and collaborative. The right-side Universal Canvas gives agents and humans shared working surfaces for browser sessions, Office files, workspace history, and other plugin panels.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe canvas makes agent work visible. You can watch it browse, inspect what changed, open files, cowork on deliverables, and intervene before a small mistake becomes a large one.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" class=\"js-gh-image-fallback\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAgent Zero includes a direct Playwright-powered Browser tool with a visible WebUI viewer. The agent can navigate pages, inspect readable page content, and act through typed page references such as \u003ccode\u003e[link 3]\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003e[button 6]\u003c/code\u003e, and \u003ccode\u003e[input text 8]\u003c/code\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor web and mobile development, Annotate mode lets you click page elements or regions and leave actionable comments for the agent targeted at the page itself. You can review a UI visually, mark what needs to change, and send those notes straight back into the conversation.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThe Browser also supports Chrome extensions installed from the Chrome Web Store directly inside the Agent Zero browser environment, so workflows can use the same kind of browser capabilities real users depend on.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eUse Your OpenAI Codex Plan\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-use-your-openai-codex-plan\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Use Your OpenAI Codex Plan\" href=\"#use-your-openai-codex-plan\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAgent Zero can now connect to your OpenAI Codex plan through the new OAuth flow. Sign in with your account, pick the Codex-backed provider, and let Agent Zero use the plan you already have.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/res/oauth_llm_plans-ok.png\"\u003e\u003cimg width=\"1184\" height=\"604\" alt=\"OAuth LLM plans in Agent Zero\" src=\"/agent0ai/agent-zero/raw/main/docs/res/oauth_llm_plans-ok.png\" style=\"max-width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 604px;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eClick \"Connect\", enter the device code in the OpenAI page. Choose your model after checking the list, and you're all set.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis is the first step toward account-backed LLM plans in Agent Zero. More integrations are coming, including Gemini CLI, Claude Code based on extra-usage, and more.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch1 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eA0 CLI Connector: Use Agent Zero on Your Host Machine\u003c/h1\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-a0-cli-connector-use-agent-zero-on-your-host-machine\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: A0 CLI Connector: Use Agent Zero on Your Host Machine\" href=\"#a0-cli-connector-use-agent-zero-on-your-host-machine\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAgent Zero is safe when it lives in Docker. class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"a0\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003ea0\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ccode\u003ea0\u003c/code\u003e connects your terminal to an Agent Zero instance. It can usually discover a local instance automatically, or you can point it at a remote Agent Zero URL hosted somewhere else, such as a VPS or tunnel.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eWhen you activate \u003cstrong\u003eRead+Write\u003c/strong\u003e access and the \u003cstrong\u003eRemote Code Execution Tool\u003c/strong\u003e in the CLI, Agent Zero can operate on the filesystem and shell of the machine where \u003ccode\u003ea0\u003c/code\u003e is running. That means it can work on your real local project files, not only files inside the Docker sandbox.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis is especially useful if you:\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eprefer CLI workflows;\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ewant Agent Zero to work in an existing local repository;\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eare running Agent Zero on a remote server;\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eneed code execution on a headless machine without using the Web UI;\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003ewant Docker isolation for Agent Zero while still granting explicit, controlled access to selected host-side work.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eFor full setup details, manual fallback installation, and remote-host tips, see the \u003ca href=\"/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/guides/a0-cli-connector.md\"\u003eA0 CLI Connector guide\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" presets. Clone a public or private Git repo into an isolated project and give the agent context that belongs to that work alone.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eSkills use the open \u003ccode\u003eSKILL.md\u003c/code\u003e standard: portable, structured capabilities that can be activated globally, per project, or for the current chat. Agent Profiles let you switch the behavior, prompt overrides, tools, extensions, and model configuration of the active agent without rewriting the whole system.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eMulti-Agent Cooperation\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-multi-agent-cooperation\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Multi-Agent Cooperation\" href=\"#multi-agent-cooperation\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eEvery agent can create subordinate agents to break down work. The superior gives tasks and receives reports; subagents keep their own contexts focused and return their findings when done.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eThis makes Agent Zero useful for research, software engineering, data analysis, plugin development, and tasks where several specialized perspectives are better than one overloaded context.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eTransparent and Extensible by Design\u003c/h3\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-transparent-and-extensible-by-design\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Transparent and Extensible by Design\" href=\"#transparent-and-extensible-by-design\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAlmost nothing is hidden. Prompts live in \u003ccode\u003eprompts/\u003c/code\u003e, tools live in \u003ccode\u003etools/\u003c/code\u003e or plugins, and built-in behavior can be inspected, changed, replaced, or extended.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAgent Zero supports plugins, MCP, A2A, custom tools, custom prompts, project-scoped configuration, environment-based deployment settings, and a Web UI designed to keep the agent's work readable in real time.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://github.com/agent0ai/space-agent\"\u003eSpace Agent\u003c/a\u003e is our newer product direction for the agent-shaped workspace: a Space the agent can reshape from inside your browser, with live demos, a desktop app, and a path to running a real server for yourself or your team.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp align=\"left\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n  \u003ca href=\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CNRHxEZ8yqs\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/agent0ai/space-agent/raw/main/.github/thumbnail.webp\" alt=\"Watch Space Agent on YouTube\" width=\"560\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIf you want the raw power and deep customizability of an agent with a full Linux system, start here with Agent Zero. If you want the polished Space experience for easier personal, team, desktop, or self-hosted use, explore \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/agent0ai/space-agent\"\u003eSpace Agent\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eTime Travel (powered by Space Agent)\u003c/h2\u003e\u003ca id=\"user-content-time-travel-powered-by-space-agent\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Time Travel (powered by Space Agent)\" href=\"#time-travel-powered-by-space-agent\"\u003e\u003csvg class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"\u003e\u003cpath d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"\u003e\u003c/path\u003e\u003c/svg\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eTime Travel gives Agent Zero-owned \u003ccode\u003e/a0/usr\u003c/code\u003e workspaces snapshot history, diff inspection, travel, and revert. It is designed for recoverable agent work: see what changed, compare files, inspect a past state, and roll back when needed. Try it in Space Agent as well (link above).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ca target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/res/time-travel.png\"\u003e\u003cimg alt=\"Time Travel\" src=\"/agent0ai/agent-zero/raw/main/docs/res/time-travel.png\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eIt is not a replacement for Git or backups. It is a practical safety layer for the workspace where agents are actively creating and editing files.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eSoftware engineering:\u003c/strong\u003e inspect a codebase, make scoped edits, run tests, explain tradeoffs, and keep a recoverable history of file changes.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHost-machine development:\u003c/strong\u003e connect with \u003ccode\u003ea0\u003c/code\u003e, grant Read+Write and remote execution when needed, and let Agent Zero work in your real local repositories.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFinancial analysis and charting:\u003c/strong\u003e collect data, correlate events, create spreadsheets, and generate editable charts.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOffice deliverables:\u003c/strong\u003e cowork on documents, spreadsheets, and presentation decks instead of trapping the result in chat text.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWeb and mobile QA:\u003c/strong\u003e browse an app, annotate UI issues, install browser extensions, and turn visual comments into actionable fixes.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eAPI integration:\u003c/strong\u003e paste an API snippet, let the agent build a working example, and store the pattern for future use.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eClient/project isolation:\u003c/strong\u003e keep memory, secrets, instructions, files, and model choices separated by project.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eScheduled operations:\u003c/strong\u003e run recurring checks and monitoring tasks with project-scoped context and credentials.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\"\u003eSafety Treat it with the same respect you would give a capable developer with shell access.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cul dir=\"auto\"\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eKeep it running inside Docker or another isolated environment.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eDo not mount your entire home directory unless you understand the risk.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eGrant A0 CLI Read+Write access and remote code execution only for machines and workspaces you trust.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eStore credentials in project secrets or settings, not in prompts or public files.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eReview actions that touch accounts, money, production systems, or private data.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eKeep backups for important workspaces.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003cli\u003eInstall browser extensions and third-party plugins only from sources you trust.\u003c/li\u003e\n\u003c/ul\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" account OAuth provider

Create a generic OAuth Connections plugin with Codex/ChatGPT Account as the first provider, using OpenAI's device-code flow to persist Codex-compatible account tokens.

Expose a loopback OpenAI-compatible wrapper for models, responses, and chat completions, and point LiteLLM at the container-local Agent Zero origin.

Add a dummy API-key extension and focused tests so the account-backed provider appears configured without requiring a user-entered key.

docs: add Codex plan OAuth callout

Highlight that Agent Zero can use an existing OpenAI Codex plan through the new OAuth flow.

Add the account-backed LLM plans image and surface the section from the README navigation, while pointing toward future Gemini CLI and Claude Code integrations.

Handle Codex account SSE chat chunks

Teach the Codex/ChatGPT account bridge to extract text from OpenAI-style SSE chat completion deltas and fall back to a normal output_text response when upstream only streams chunks.

Strip user-supplied stream kwargs before LiteLLM calls so Agent Zero owns streaming mode and custom parameters cannot pass stream twice.

Add targeted tests for streamed delta extraction and reconstructed responses.

update README.md with LLM plans mention" class="Link--secondary" href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/commit/f67564a8aeccd4b0011069ae71803356f04add1f">Add Codex/ChatGPT account OAuth class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="browser: replace browser-use agent with native browser

Introduce the new built-in Browser plugin for Agent Zero, replacing the legacy
browser-use-based browser agent with a direct Playwright-powered browser tool,
live WebUI viewer, browser session controls, status APIs, configuration, and
extension-management support.

Add browser-specific modal behavior so the browser can run as a floating,
resizable, no-backdrop window, including modal focus, toggle, and idempotent
open helpers for richer WebUI surfaces.

Remove the old `_browser_agent` core plugin and the `browser-use` dependency,
then clean up stale browser-model wiring and references across agent code,
model configuration docs, setup guides, troubleshooting docs, skills, and
Agent Zero knowledge.

Update regression and WebUI extension-surface coverage for the new browser
architecture and modal behavior.

The legacy browser-use implementation has been extracted from core so it can
continue separately as a community plugin published through the A0 Plugin Index for any user or professional that were relying on it for workflow." class="Link--secondary" title="refactor: extract browser agent into built-in plugin

Move the Browser Agent/browser-use stack into a tracked built-in
`_browser_agent` plugin while preserving the current model/config flow.

Changes:
- add built-in `_browser_agent` plugin with tool, helpers, prompts, assets,
  status API, and WebUI message/status surfaces
- move browser-use wrapper and monkeypatch ownership out of `models.py`
  into plugin helper code
- keep browser model resolution on the `_model_config` path and continue
  using the effective Main Model / chat model config
- remove stale Browser HTTP Headers UI and outdated browser-model wording
- make Playwright runtime resolution bootstrap-only and stop installing
  browser binaries on demand
- switch browser log rendering to plugin-owned WebUI extension handling
  with core fallback compatibility for old logs
- delete obsolete core browser tool/helpers/prompts/assets after plugin
  resolution is in place
- update docs to reflect built-in plugin ownership and Main Model browser
  behavior

feat(browser-agent): improve lifecycle reliability, LLM compatibility, and local-dev bootstrap

- **Lifecycle Reliability**: Implemented stale Chromium lock file cleanup and one-shot ephemeral profile fallback to prevent CDP connection hangs and profile-locked crashes.
- **LLM Compatibility**: Added OpenRouter/Gemini shims including schema compaction, system instruction injection, and JSON output normalization for `browser-use` validation.
- **Cache Alignment**: Reverted Playwright browser cache to `tmp/playwright` for parity with core behavior and updated Docker scripts and documentation accordingly.
- **Local-Dev Bootstrap**: Added a plugin `install()` hook to automate Python dependency setup and transitioned bootstrap feedback to UI notifications.
- **Plugin Config**: Set `always_enabled: false` to allow users to opt-out or bring their own browser providers.
- **Testing**: Expanded test suite to cover lifecycle edge cases, LLM normalization, and bootstrap logic.

rework clean up browser agent sessions on reset/removal

Force browser-agent teardown to use BrowserSession.kill() so keep_alive sessions do not leave Chromium running and keep the profile locked. Add centralized browser state cleanup for reset/context removal and regression tests to cover the new teardown path and prevent SingletonLock fallbacks from stale sessions.

add browser agent thumbnail

restore local dev for Playwright in browser agent via hook

- Introduced a new hook to bootstrap Playwright for local development, ensuring the Chromium headless shell is installed when in development mode.
- Updated the Playwright helper to call the new hook if the binary is not found during the binary check.
- Added tests to verify the correct installation and behavior of the Playwright binary in local development scenarios.

proper install notifications for Playwright in local dev

- Added notification management for Playwright bootstrap process, including info, success, and error notifications.
- Enhanced the `bootstrap_local_dev_playwright` function to notify users about the installation status of the Playwright runtime.
- Updated tests to verify the correct notifications are sent during the Playwright installation process.

rework browser state cleanup extensions for agent context removal and reset

- Added `CleanupBrowserStateOnRemove` and `CleanupBrowserStateOnReset` extensions to handle browser state cleanup when an agent context is removed or reset.
- Updated `cleanup_browser_agent_state` function to utilize new protocols for better type safety and clarity.
- Enhanced the `State` class to ensure proper cleanup of browser sessions and user data directories.
- Introduced tests to validate the cleanup functionality and ensure browser sessions are correctly managed during agent lifecycle events.

enhance Playwright cache handling

- Updated Playwright helper to include support for a new cache directory at `.cache/ms-playwright`, aligning with standard cache locations.
- Modified the `get_playwright_search_dirs` function to incorporate the new cache path.
- Added a new test to verify the retrieval of the Playwright binary from the repository's `.cache/ms-playwright` directory, ensuring proper functionality across different cache locations.
- Updated existing tests to reflect changes in cache path handling.

update docs and README.md

restore local dev bootstrap and async-safe teardown

Keep Browser Agent bootstrap local in development, matching pre-extraction behavior, by removing the RFC filesystem hop from Playwright cache setup. Restore on-demand local-dev bootstrap through plugin hooks before browser-use import, so missing browser packages and runtime can be installed on first use.

Rework browser session teardown to be async-safe during reset and cleanup, avoiding nested event loop shutdown failures while still terminating the browser worker cleanly. Also remove redundant browser-use patch application and add regression tests covering local bootstrap, reset-time async shutdown, and lifecycle cleanup.

playwright bootstrap via plugin hooks

[Fixed][P1] The hook-based restore was architecturally in the right place, but it was too late to recover a missing local browser_use / playwright install. The browser tool imports browser_use at module load time, and tool loading silently skips modules that fail to import, so the plugin install() hook could never rescue the first browser invocation if those packages were absent. I fixed that by calling the plugin install hook before importing browser_use in local development: browser_use.py, browser_use.py, agent.py, test_browser_agent_playwright_bootstrap.py.

[Fixed][P3] The Browser Agent UI and runtime error text still claimed on-demand install did not exist, which contradicted the intended local-dev bootstrap path. I corrected both messages so they now describe the real behavior: main.html, playwright.py.

rm tests

restore old browser use logic files

restore old browser working behaviour

browser: migrate cleanup hooks to new extensible paths

Update Browser Agent cleanup extensions to the new deep @extensible path layout introduced in 7e1d9ad2a4a3d186e337a89433ccc5128bdc754d, so AgentContext reset/remove hooks fire again after the framework migration.

`This restores browser state cleanup when chats are reset or deleted, preventing keep-alive browser sessions from surviving context removal and leaving chats stuck until restart. The change also keeps the recent browser regressions covered with focused tests for Anthropic/OpenRouter action normalization, keep-alive session teardown via kill(), and extension discovery under the new _functions/... path structure.`

Update messages.js

Add a builtin `a0-setup-cli` skill for guiding host-side A0 connector setup,
and restore the lightweight trigger-word based skill matching flow, which many users asked for.

- add builtin `skills/a0-setup-cli/` with installer-first host setup guidance,
  container guardrails, fallback install paths, and example responses
- fix `helpers.skills_cli` so builtin skills under `/skills` are discoverable,
  searchable, and validatable
- restore trigger-pattern scoring in runtime `search_skills()`
- re-enable `skills_tool:search` in the current tool flow
- add lightweight lexical relevant-skill recall for the current user message
  without reintroducing memory/vector-db skill recall
- update skill prompts to steer the agent toward search/load when requests
  match skill trigger phrases" class="Link--secondary" href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/commit/954eca35635388207bc8d4ae39a1c73ec0d23a38">add a0-setup-cli Skill and restore lexical trigger Keep image payloads out of persistent agent history by storing vision and
computer-use captures as file path references instead of inline base64 data.

- update vision_load to attach image paths without compression or JPEG conversion
- update computer_use_remote to attach shared capture artifact paths directly
- serialize local image refs into provider-valid data URLs only at request prep
- reject base64/data URL attachments on the connector WebSocket path
- advertise path_or_url as the connector attachment mode" class="Link--secondary" href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/commit/1993f6f86415a0352d49eda61d3c061eb4b5dcea">Store vision and computer-use images as Date:   Tue Mar 31 08:49:15 2026 +0800

    fix: auto-reinstall corrupt node_modules and stop poll loop after repeated bridge failures

    _ensure_npm_install now verifies key package exists, not just
    node_modules dir. Wipes and reinstalls if corrupt.

    Poll loop stops after 5 consecutive bridge start failures instead
    of spamming errors and making A0 unusable.

commit 61fa1bf4870d7dc0c335bc4ec68f44470d2e8099

    fix: move allowed_numbers filtering from JS bridge to Python handler

    The JS bridge used LIDs (internal WhatsApp identifiers) for sender
    matching which never matched actual phone numbers. Moved filtering
    to Python handler.py where config is read fresh each poll cycle.

    - Add senderNumber (resolved phone) to bridge message payload
    - Filter in poll_messages() with normalized number comparison
    - Remove --allowed-numbers CLI arg and JS-side filtering
    - Fix ensure_bridge_http_up not recording _bridge_config

account OAuth provider

Create a generic OAuth Connections plugin with Codex/ChatGPT Account as the first provider, using OpenAI's device-code flow to persist Codex-compatible account tokens.

Expose a loopback OpenAI-compatible wrapper for models, responses, and chat completions, and point LiteLLM at the container-local Agent Zero origin.

Add a dummy API-key extension and focused tests so the account-backed provider appears configured without requiring a user-entered key.

docs: add Codex plan OAuth callout

Highlight that Agent Zero can use an existing OpenAI Codex plan through the new OAuth flow.

Add the account-backed LLM plans image and surface the section from the README navigation, while pointing toward future Gemini CLI and Claude Code integrations.

Handle Codex account SSE chat chunks

Teach the Codex/ChatGPT account bridge to extract text from OpenAI-style SSE chat completion deltas and fall back to a normal output_text response when upstream only streams chunks.

Strip user-supplied stream kwargs before LiteLLM calls so Agent Zero owns streaming mode and custom parameters cannot pass stream twice.

Add targeted tests for streamed delta extraction and reconstructed responses.

update README.md with LLM plans mention" class="Link--secondary" href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/commit/f67564a8aeccd4b0011069ae71803356f04add1f">Add Codex/ChatGPT account OAuth cache & add API extension hooks

Add extension hooks and improve plugin cache handling. Changes include:

- Add new JSON API extension points and a cache reset handler (extensions/webui/json_api_call_before, json_api_call_after/cache_reset.js) to clear frontend cache when backend cache_reset runs.
- Rename webui extension hook directories to use fetch_api_call_* naming.
- Refactor webui/js/api.js to normalize API URLs, lazily import ./extensions.js, and call extension hooks for json_api_call_before/error/after and fetch_api_call_before/after using a ctx object.
- Rename invalidate_plugin_cache to clear_plugin_cache and call it after plugin install/delete/toggle/config save to keep plugin cache consistent.
- Harden plugin name sanitization to replace non-alphanumeric chars with underscores.
- Include extensions/**/*.js in jsconfig.json for editor tooling.

These changes improve extensibility for API lifecycle events and ensure plugin-related cache is cleared when plugin state changes." class="Link--secondary" docs: add Codex plan OAuth callout

Handle Codex account SSE chat chunks

Teach the Codex/ChatGPT account bridge to extract text from OpenAI-style SSE chat completion deltas and fall back to a normal output_text response when upstream only streams chunks.

Strip user-supplied stream kwargs before LiteLLM calls so Agent Zero owns streaming mode and custom parameters cannot pass stream twice.

Add targeted tests for streamed delta extraction and reconstructed responses.

replace browser-use agent with native browser

Introduce the new built-in Browser plugin for Agent Zero, replacing the legacy
browser-use-based browser agent with a direct Playwright-powered browser tool,
live WebUI viewer, browser session controls, status APIs, configuration, and
extension-management support.

Add browser-specific modal behavior so the browser can run as a floating,
resizable, no-backdrop window, including modal focus, toggle, and idempotent
open helpers for richer WebUI surfaces.

Remove the old `_browser_agent` core plugin and the `browser-use` dependency,
then clean up stale browser-model wiring and references across agent code,
model configuration docs, setup guides, troubleshooting docs, skills, and
Agent Zero knowledge.

Update regression and WebUI extension-surface coverage for the new browser
architecture and modal behavior.

The legacy browser-use implementation has been extracted from core so it can
continue separately as a community plugin published through the A0 Plugin Index for any user or professional that were relying on it for workflow." class="Link--secondary" <div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Agent Zero</h1><a id="user-content-agent-zero" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Agent Zero" href="#agent-zero"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">AI agents with a full Linux system at their fingertips, and yours.</h3><a id="user-content-ai-agents-with-a-full-linux-system-at-their-fingertips-and-yours" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: AI agents with a full Linux system at their fingertips, and yours." href="#ai-agents-with-a-full-linux-system-at-their-fingertips-and-yours"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero is a dynamic, organic agentic framework for running AI agents that can create tools, write code, browse the web, cooperate with other agents, and keep learning from your goals and projects.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://agent-zero.ai" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/ad5d3faf11b8b5800ee770a8de5fd1e1a281d5170c41e315571be8d94340c022/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f576562736974652d6167656e742d2d7a65726f2e61692d3041313932463f7374796c653d666f722d7468652d6261646765266c6f676f3d76657263656c266c6f676f436f6c6f723d7768697465" alt="Website" data-canonical-src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Website-agent--zero.ai-0A192F?style=for-the-badge&logo=vercel&logoColor=white" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What Is Agent Zero</h1><a id="user-content-what-is-agent-zero" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What Is Agent Zero" href="#what-is-agent-zero"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero is not a predefined one-purpose agent.</p>
<p dir="auto">It is a transparent, extensible framework where the agent can use the operating system as a tool: a real Linux environment, terminal, code execution, files, memory, browser automation, plugins, and tools it learns to create along the way.</p>
<p dir="auto">The goal is simple: give an AI agent enough environment, memory, communication, and freedom to solve real tasks while keeping the work inspectable and steerable by you.</p>

<p dir="auto">Then open the Web UI, configure your LLM provider, and start with a concrete task. For the full setup path, including updates and platform notes, see the <a href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/setup/installation.md">Installation guide</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">What Makes Agent Zero Different</h1><a id="user-content-what-makes-agent-zero-different" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: What Makes Agent Zero Different" href="#what-makes-agent-zero-different"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Computer as a Tool</h2><a id="user-content-computer-as-a-tool" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Computer as a Tool" href="#computer-as-a-tool"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero can use a Kali Linux system to accomplish your task. It can inspect files, run commands, write code, install and use tools, create scripts, search the web, and adapt its workflow as the task evolves.</p>
<p dir="auto">The important idea is not a fixed list of buttons. The important idea is that the agent can build and use the right tool when the work demands it.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Universal Canvas</h2><a id="user-content-universal-canvas" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Universal Canvas" href="#universal-canvas"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero is becoming more visual and collaborative. The right-side Universal Canvas gives agents and humans shared working surfaces for browser sessions, Office files, workspace history, and other plugin panels.</p>
<p dir="auto">The canvas makes agent work visible. You can watch it browse, inspect what changed, open files, cowork on deliverables, and intervene before a small mistake becomes a large one.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Cowork on Office Documents</h2><a id="user-content-cowork-on-office-documents" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Cowork on Office Documents" href="#cowork-on-office-documents"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Create, open, and cowork with the AI on documents, spreadsheets, and presentation decks.</p>

<p dir="auto">The Office canvas supports editable artifacts with Collabora Online and WOPI, including DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX workflows. Agents can create substantial deliverables, read their contents, apply precise saved edits, preserve version history, and generate native XLSX charts directly inside spreadsheets.</p>

<br>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero includes a direct Playwright-powered Browser tool with a visible WebUI viewer. The agent can navigate pages, inspect readable page content, and act through typed page references such as <code>[link 3]</code>, <code>[button 6]</code>, and <code>[input text 8]</code>.</p>
<p dir="auto">For web and mobile development, Annotate mode lets you click page elements or regions and leave actionable comments for the agent targeted at the page itself. You can review a UI visually, mark what needs to change, and send those notes straight back into the conversation.</p>
<p dir="auto">The Browser also supports Chrome extensions installed from the Chrome Web Store directly inside the Agent Zero browser environment, so workflows can use the same kind of browser capabilities real users depend on.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Use Your OpenAI Codex Plan</h2><a id="user-content-use-your-openai-codex-plan" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Use Your OpenAI Codex Plan" href="#use-your-openai-codex-plan"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero can now connect to your OpenAI Codex plan through the new OAuth flow. Sign in with your account, pick the Codex-backed provider, and let Agent Zero use the plan you already have.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/res/oauth_llm_plans-ok.png"><img width="1184" height="604" alt="OAuth LLM plans in Agent Zero" src="/agent0ai/agent-zero/raw/main/docs/res/oauth_llm_plans-ok.png" style="max-width: 100%; height: auto; max-height: 604px;"></a></p>
<br>
<p dir="auto">Click "Connect", enter the device code in the OpenAI page. Choose your model after checking the list, and you're all set.</p>
<p dir="auto">This is the first step toward account-backed LLM plans in Agent Zero. More integrations are coming, including Gemini CLI, Claude Code based on extra-usage, and more.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h1 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">A0 CLI Connector: Use Agent Zero on Your Host Machine</h1><a id="user-content-a0-cli-connector-use-agent-zero-on-your-host-machine" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: A0 CLI Connector: Use Agent Zero on Your Host Machine" href="#a0-cli-connector-use-agent-zero-on-your-host-machine"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero is safe when it lives in Docker. The <strong>A0 CLI Connector</strong> is how you intentionally let it work beyond the container: on your host machine, in a terminal-first workflow, or against a server where you do not want a GUI at all.</p>

<p dir="auto">Install the connector on the machine you want Agent Zero to work on, not inside the Agent Zero container.</p>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Windows PowerShell</h3><a id="user-content-windows-powershell-1" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Windows PowerShell" href="#windows-powershell-1"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<p dir="auto">Then run:</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>a0</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

      <svg aria-hidden="true" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<p dir="auto"><code>a0</code> connects your terminal to an Agent Zero instance. It can usually discover a local instance automatically, or you can point it at a remote Agent Zero URL hosted somewhere else, such as a VPS or tunnel.</p>
<p dir="auto">When you activate <strong>Read+Write</strong> access and the <strong>Remote Code Execution Tool</strong> in the CLI, Agent Zero can operate on the filesystem and shell of the machine where <code>a0</code> is running. That means it can work on your real local project files, not only files inside the Docker sandbox.</p>
<p dir="auto">This is especially useful if you:</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>prefer CLI workflows;</li>
<li>want Agent Zero to work in an existing local repository;</li>
<li>are running Agent Zero on a remote server;</li>
<li>need code execution on a headless machine without using the Web UI;</li>
<li>want Docker isolation for Agent Zero while still granting explicit, controlled access to selected host-side work.</li>
</ul>
<p dir="auto">For full setup details, manual fallback installation, and remote-host tips, see the <a href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/guides/a0-cli-connector.md">A0 CLI Connector guide</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Projects, Skills, and Agent Profiles</h3><a id="user-content-projects-skills-and-agent-profiles" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Projects, Skills, and Agent Profiles" href="#projects-skills-and-agent-profiles"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Projects isolate workspaces, instructions, memory, secrets, knowledge, repositories, and model presets. Clone a public or private Git repo into an isolated project and give the agent context that belongs to that work alone.</p>
<p dir="auto">Skills use the open <code>SKILL.md</code> standard: portable, structured capabilities that can be activated globally, per project, or for the current chat. Agent Profiles let you switch the behavior, prompt overrides, tools, extensions, and model configuration of the active agent without rewriting the whole system.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Multi-Agent Cooperation</h3><a id="user-content-multi-agent-cooperation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Multi-Agent Cooperation" href="#multi-agent-cooperation"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Every agent can create subordinate agents to break down work. The superior gives tasks and receives reports; subagents keep their own contexts focused and return their findings when done.</p>
<p dir="auto">This makes Agent Zero useful for research, software engineering, data analysis, plugin development, and tasks where several specialized perspectives are better than one overloaded context.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Transparent and Extensible by Design</h3><a id="user-content-transparent-and-extensible-by-design" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Transparent and Extensible by Design" href="#transparent-and-extensible-by-design"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Almost nothing is hidden. Prompts live in <code>prompts/</code>, tools live in <code>tools/</code> or plugins, and built-in behavior can be inspected, changed, replaced, or extended.</p>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero supports plugins, MCP, A2A, custom tools, custom prompts, project-scoped configuration, environment-based deployment settings, and a Web UI designed to keep the agent's work readable in real time.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Also Included</h3><a id="user-content-also-included" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Also Included" href="#also-included"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Fully Dockerized runtime with a clean Web UI.</li>
<li>Real-time streamed output so you can interrupt, redirect, or refine the work as it happens.</li>
<li>Speech-to-text and text-to-speech support.</li>
<li>Chat load/save, generated HTML logs, file browser, settings UI, and deployment-friendly <code>A0_SET_</code> configuration.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Try These First</h2><a id="user-content-try-these-first" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Try These First" href="#try-these-first"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Research with a browser:</strong> "Open the browser, compare three project management tools for a small AI team, and summarize the tradeoffs with source links."</li>
<li><strong>Cowork on a spreadsheet:</strong> "Create an editable XLSX budget model with assumptions, monthly projections, and a native chart."</li>
<li><strong>Review a web UI:</strong> "Open my local app in the Browser. I will annotate the page with comments; then implement the requested UI fixes."</li>
<li><strong>Work inside a Git project:</strong> "Clone this repository into a new project, inspect the architecture, and propose the safest first improvement."</li>
<li><strong>Create a specialist:</strong> "Create an Agent Profile for financial analysis with cautious reasoning, clear assumptions, and spreadsheet-first deliverables."</li>
<li><strong>Recover a workspace:</strong> "Show me recent Time Travel snapshots and explain what changed before I revert anything."</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Agent Zero and Space Agent</h2><a id="user-content-agent-zero-and-space-agent" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Agent Zero and Space Agent" href="#agent-zero-and-space-agent"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero is the open framework and Linux-powered agent workbench.</p>
<p dir="auto"><a href="https://github.com/agent0ai/space-agent">Space Agent</a> is our newer product direction for the agent-shaped workspace: a Space the agent can reshape from inside your browser, with live demos, a desktop app, and a path to running a real server for yourself or your team.</p>
<p align="left" dir="auto">
  <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CNRHxEZ8yqs" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://github.com/agent0ai/space-agent/raw/main/.github/thumbnail.webp" alt="Watch Space Agent on YouTube" width="560" style="max-width: 100%;"></a>
</p>
<p dir="auto">If you want the raw power and deep customizability of an agent with a full Linux system, start here with Agent Zero. If you want the polished Space experience for easier personal, team, desktop, or self-hosted use, explore <a href="https://github.com/agent0ai/space-agent">Space Agent</a>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Time Travel (powered by Space Agent)</h2><a id="user-content-time-travel-powered-by-space-agent" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Time Travel (powered by Space Agent)" href="#time-travel-powered-by-space-agent"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Time Travel gives Agent Zero-owned <code>/a0/usr</code> workspaces snapshot history, diff inspection, travel, and revert. It is designed for recoverable agent work: see what changed, compare files, inspect a past state, and roll back when needed. Try it in Space Agent as well (link above).</p>
<p dir="auto"><a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/res/time-travel.png"><img alt="Time Travel" src="/agent0ai/agent-zero/raw/main/docs/res/time-travel.png" style="max-width: 100%;"></a></p>
<p dir="auto">It is not a replacement for Git or backups. It is a practical safety layer for the workspace where agents are actively creating and editing files.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Real-World Use Cases</h2><a id="user-content-real-world-use-cases" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Real-World Use Cases" href="#real-world-use-cases"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<ul dir="auto">
<li><strong>Software engineering:</strong> inspect a codebase, make scoped edits, run tests, explain tradeoffs, and keep a recoverable history of file changes.</li>
<li><strong>Host-machine development:</strong> connect with <code>a0</code>, grant Read+Write and remote execution when needed, and let Agent Zero work in your real local repositories.</li>
<li><strong>Financial analysis and charting:</strong> collect data, correlate events, create spreadsheets, and generate editable charts.</li>
<li><strong>Office deliverables:</strong> cowork on documents, spreadsheets, and presentation decks instead of trapping the result in chat text.</li>
<li><strong>Web and mobile QA:</strong> browse an app, annotate UI issues, install browser extensions, and turn visual comments into actionable fixes.</li>
<li><strong>API integration:</strong> paste an API snippet, let the agent build a working example, and store the pattern for future use.</li>
<li><strong>Client/project isolation:</strong> keep memory, secrets, instructions, files, and model choices separated by project.</li>
<li><strong>Scheduled operations:</strong> run recurring checks and monitoring tasks with project-scoped context and credentials.</li>
</ul>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Safety Model</h2><a id="user-content-safety-model" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Safety Model" href="#safety-model"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero is powerful because it can use a real environment. Treat it with the same respect you would give a capable developer with shell access.</p>
<ul dir="auto">
<li>Keep it running inside Docker or another isolated environment.</li>
<li>Do not mount your entire home directory unless you understand the risk.</li>
<li>Grant A0 CLI Read+Write access and remote code execution only for machines and workspaces you trust.</li>
<li>Store credentials in project secrets or settings, not in prompts or public files.</li>
<li>Review actions that touch accounts, money, production systems, or private data.</li>
<li>Keep backups for important workspaces.</li>
<li>Install browser extensions and third-party plugins only from sources you trust.</li>
</ul>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Build With Us</h2><a id="user-content-build-with-us" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Build With Us" href="#build-with-us"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">Agent Zero is built for people who want to understand and shape their tools.</p>
<p dir="auto">You can help by improving docs, creating skills, publishing plugins, testing model/provider setups, reporting bugs, sharing workflows, or contributing core improvements. Start with the <a href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/guides/contribution.md">Contributing guide</a>, browse the <a href="/agent0ai/agent-zero/blob/main/docs/guides/usage.md">Plugin Hub</a>, or bring ideas to Discord.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Community and Support</h2><a id="user-content-community-and-support" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Community and Support" href="#community-and-support"><svg class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
